title: Docker Image Tag Validation Bug Fixer

statement.markdown: Write a function that validates a Docker image tag. A valid Docker image tag must start with a letter followed by a period, followed by a string of digits, letters, and underscores. The function should return True if the tag is valid and False otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the Docker image tag.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the tag is valid.
statement.notes: Assume that the input string is not empty.

examples.explanation: The tag starts with a letter, followed by a period, followed by a string of digits, letters, and underscores.
The tag does not end with a digit.

hints.text: Check the pattern of the tag to see if it matches the required format.

editorial.title: Solving the Docker Image Tag Validation Bug
editorial.intuition: Use a regular expression to match the pattern of the tag.
editorial.bruteForce: Check the first character of the tag and then check the rest of the string.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a regular expression to match the pattern of the tag.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a regular expression to match the pattern of the tag.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The regular expression will match the pattern of the tag if and only if the tag is valid.
editorial.dryRun: Example run: Input: alpine:3.14.0, Output: True
commonMistakes: Not checking the rest of the string after matching the first character.
Not using a regular expression to match the pattern of the tag.
